solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int smallestRepunitDivByK(int k) {
        if (k % 2 == 0 || k % 5 == 0) return -1;
        int remainder = 0;
        for (int length = 1; length <= k; length++) {
            remainder = (remainder * 10 + 1) % k;
            if (remainder == 0) return length;
        }
        return -1;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(k)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution leverages modular arithmetic to avoid constructing large numbers directly. It iteratively builds the remainder of numbers composed of only 1s modulo k. If a remainder of 0 is found, the length is returned. If k is divisible by 2 or 5, it is impossible to find such an n, so we return -1 immediately.
